Bus Timings

16 RTA Bus Timings - Rashidiya Bus Station to Al Awir, CMC

July 16, 2025
By Al Khaleej
Install
★★★★★ 5.0
Google Play Official App
UAE Bus Timing

Travel across the UAE with confidence. No confusion. No wasted time.

This route offers a seamless blend of comfort, affordability, and convenience, making it an appealing choice for those looking to navigate the city with ease.

Operational Details

The 16 bus runs daily, beginning its service at 6:00 AM from Rashidiya Bus Station and continuing until around 10:00 PM, catering to a variety of travel needs, from work and school to leisure activities.

From Rashidiya Bus Station From Al Awir, CMC
06:00 05:00
07:10 06:00
08:20 07:10
09:35 08:25
10:40 09:35
11:20 10:50
12:10 11:50
12:55 12:30
13:45 13:30
14:35 14:05
15:10 14:55
16:00 15:45
17:20 16:15
18:20 17:10
19:45 18:35
20:55 19:40
22:05 21:00
22:05  

Bus 16 Timetable

For the latest schedule, passengers are encouraged to check the official RTA website or the 16 RTA Bus Timings.

Ticket Pricing: The fare for the 16 bus journey is conveniently set at AED 3 within one zone when using a Silver or Personal Nol card. Payments are processed through Dubai’s cashless Nol card system, which can be easily topped up at various locations throughout the city, ensuring a seamless travel experience that is especially beneficial for daily commuters.

Route Overview

The 16 bus route begins at Rashidiya Bus Station and makes several key stops along the way, providing access to important areas and community hubs. Notable stops include:

  1. Rashidiya Bus Station Parking: The starting point of the journey, offering connections to various transport options for travelers.

  2. Rashidiya Bus Station: A key hub that connects passengers to multiple routes across the city.

  3. Mushrif, Eppco: A convenient stop for fuel and refreshments.

4.

5.

  1. Mizhar 2, Turnoff: A useful turning point for local access.

  2. ناديلوتاهللفروسية (Lutah Equestrian Club): A unique stop for equestrian enthusiasts.

  3. Khawaneej Road, 2: A key intersection for local traffic.

  4. Al Khawaneej Road 4: Another crucial stop along the route, providing access to nearby areas.

  5. Amardi Boys School 2: Serving the local school community.

  6. Khawaneej Police Station: A helpful landmark for travelers needing assistance, ensuring safety and support along the route.

  7. Palace Masjid: A prominent mosque that serves the local community.

  8. Faisal Stud: A point of interest for horse lovers and those interested in local breeding.

  9. Al Athbaa Primary School: Catering to the educational needs of the area.

  10. Bin Bisher Farm: A stop that showcases local agriculture and farming practices.

  11. Khawaneej, Boys School: Another educational institution serving the community.

  12. Rawabi Diary, Crossing: An intersection that connects local traffic.

  13. Imam Al Shafi Masjid, Amardi: A mosque providing religious services to the community.

Recommended Guides

Enhance your daily commute with these essential transport guides:

Complete RTA Intercity Routes Guide Largest Bus Stations Infrastructure in UAE

  1. Amardi, Road: A local road providing access to various residential areas.

  2. Amardi, Road 4: Another road connecting the local neighborhoods.

  3. Etisalat: A key telecommunications hub for residents.

  4. CMC Roundabout: A central junction that connects multiple routes, facilitating convenient transfers for passengers heading to different destinations.

  5. CMC Road: A major thoroughfare for local traffic.

  6. Hissa Bint Hamdan Al Maktoums Stable: A unique stop for those interested in equestrian activities.

  7. Awir, Palace: An area of local interest with cultural significance.

  8. Waha School: Another educational institution serving the community.

  9. CMC Road 3: Connecting local neighborhoods to key areas.

  10. CMC Road 4: Further facilitating local traffic flow.

  11. CMC Masab Bin Umair Masjid: A mosque serving the local population.

  12. Awir, CMC: The final destination, providing access to residential areas and local services, making it a vital hub for the community.

Accessibility Features

The 16 bus service is designed with accessibility in mind. The fleet includes low-floor buses for easy boarding, designated seating for passengers with disabilities, and audio-visual aids to assist those with hearing or vision impairments. These features ensure that the 16 route provides an inclusive and comfortable travel experience for all passengers.

Connectivity and Transfer Points

One of the strengths of the 16 bus route is its connectivity with other public transport options. At Rashidiya Bus Station, passengers can easily transfer to other bus routes, enhancing their travel flexibility. The bus also links with local services at Al Awir, allowing for convenient onward journeys to various destinations.

Nearby Attractions and Landmarks

  1. Mushrif Park: Perfect for family outings and outdoor activities.

  2. Faisal Stud: A unique spot for equestrian enthusiasts.

Journey Experience

Taking the bus on route 16 from Rashidiya Bus Station to Al Awir, CMC offers a delightful journey through Dubai’s vibrant neighborhoods.

It culminates at Al Awir, CMC, providing easy access to local amenities and services, making this trip both a practical commute and a chance to experience the diverse community and attractions of Dubai.

Tips and Travel Advice

To ensure a smooth journey, travelers are encouraged to:

  • Ensure their Nol card is sufficiently loaded before boarding to avoid delays.
  • Utilize the RTA app for real-time updates and bus schedules.

Bus 16, an Urban Bus of RTA Dubai, showcases the city’s dedication to efficient and accessible public transport. With strategic stops, affordable fares, and inclusive features, this route serves a diverse range of travelers, from daily commuters to tourists exploring the city. For the latest updates and information, passengers are encouraged to visit the RTA website or use the RTA app. Check out the full RTA Dubai bus route for more travel options!

Official UAE Sources

For the latest schedules, fares, and policy updates, verify with these primary sources:

  • RTA Journey Planner
  • RTA Intercity Timetable Reference (E100)
  • Abu Dhabi Mobility
  • Sharjah Roads and Transport Authority (SRTA)

Editorial review date: July 16, 2025.
